operating_state: Total net sales increased 11% to $123.9 billion driven primarily by iPhone growth from new models launched in Q4 2021, Services expansion led by advertising and App Store, and Mac strength from MacBook Pro and Air. Regional performance was broadly positive with particular strength in Greater China and Rest of Asia Pacific, while Japan declined due to lower iPhone and iPad sales. Product mix shifts and foreign currency strength contributed to revenue expansion.
cost_structure: Total gross margin percentage expanded to 43.8% from 39.8% driven by favorable product mix and foreign currency strength. Products gross margin improved to 38.4% from 35.1% due to mix dynamics, while Services gross margin increased to 72.4% from 68.4% reflecting different Services mix and leverage partially offset by higher costs. Operating expenses remained stable at 10% of sales with growth driven by headcount expansion and engineering programs.
material_events: The company launched MacBook Pro models with M1 Pro and M1 Max chips, third generation AirPods, and Apple Watch Series 7 during the quarter. Share repurchases totaled $20.4 billion with dividend payments of $3.7 billion executed. Alex Gorsky was appointed to the Board of Directors in November 2021, expanding board size to nine members.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continues affecting operations with retail stores operating at limited capacity and supply chain disruptions from component suppliers and logistics providers resulting in supply shortages impacting worldwide sales. Manufacturing purchase obligations increased to $47.6 billion representing primarily noncancelable commitments covering demand periods.
forward_outlook: The company maintains its capital return program with quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share and intent to increase dividends annually subject to Board approval. Management continues focused R&D investments deemed critical for future growth and competitive positioning in new product development. Current cash balances, operational cash generation, and debt market access will satisfy requirements over the next 12 months and beyond.
uncertainty_profile: Future gross margins remain subject to volatility and downward pressure from various factors. COVID-19 pandemic impact extent remains uncertain depending on variant emergence, vaccine effectiveness, protective measures, and global economic effects on consumer demand. No material changes to critical accounting estimates or risk factors were reported since the prior annual filing.
